source.author: SSA Catalog
source.notes: Foundational yogurt-based shawarma marinade base (concentrate) to adapt for chicken, lamb, beef, and vegetables.

ingredients.label: Lemon zest
notes: About 2 tablespoons; optional but brightens flavor.

ingredients.label: Onion powder
notes: About 1 tablespoon. Sub: 120 g grated onion (shorter storage).

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: This is a base concentrate; adjust per application if you plan to salt separately.

ingredients.label: Mild chili powder (or cayenne for heat)
notes: For spicier: use 1–2 g cayenne instead.

ingredients.label: Honey (optional)
notes: About 2 teaspoons; balances tang and improves browning.

steps.title: Mix the base concentrate
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, and optional lemon zest until smooth.
Whisk in garlic and onion powder.
Add all spices, black pepper, salt, chili powder/cayenne, and optional honey. Whisk until evenly blended.

steps.title: How to use (scaling notes)
instructions: This is a base designed to be adapted per protein/veg and batch size.
Typical usage: 180–300 g base per 900 g–1.4 kg (2–3 lb) protein or vegetables (enough to coat generously).
For chicken: use as-is. For extra tang, add 5–15 ml more lemon juice.
For lamb/beef: add 15–30 ml olive oil for a richer coating, or add 10–15 ml vinegar if you want sharper acidity.
For vegetables: thin with 15–30 ml water or lemon juice so it coats evenly without clumping.

steps.title: Marinate time guidance
instructions: Chicken: 2–12 hours (best 6–10). Avoid much beyond 24 hours (can soften texture).
Lamb/Beef: 2–12 hours (best 6–12). If very thin slices, 1–4 hours is enough.

steps.title: Storage
instructions: Store base in a sealed container in the refrigerator.
Use within 3–5 days for best freshness (garlic + dairy).
If you substituted fresh grated onion, use within 2–3 days.

steps.title: Food safety
instructions: If the base touches raw meat, do not reuse it unless you boil it thoroughly (boiling may split dairy). Safer: discard.
Always marinate proteins in the refrigerator.
